'''Tarron Neb''' was a member of a [[Tarron Neb's species|species]] that [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from the [[Mountain/Legends|mountainous]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 8/Legends|Yavin 8]], located within the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]]. When [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]<ref name="daa" /> of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="rots">{{Film|III}}</ref> became disgusted by the Yavin 8 natives, he ordered their destruction. His will was executed, and although the species was largely wiped out, Neb escaped the purge, leaving them as its sole survivor. For armament, Neb carried a [[Sword/Legends|sword]] and wore a form of [[Armor/Legends|armor]].<ref name="daa" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tarron Neb was created by [[Stephen Baker]], a 16-year-old ''[[Star Wars]]'' fan who participated in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy''{{'s}}]] "[[Design An Alien]]" competition, which was initiated in the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|third issue]] in [[1995]]. When the winning entries were published in the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|seventh issue]] in [[1996]], Baker's entry was placed under the "honorable mentions" section of the "under 16" subdivision. Although the entry was published as "Tarron," Baker's original artwork features the spelling of "Torron." ''Star Wars'' continuity editor [[Leland Chee]] later stated that successfully published "Design an Alien" material was considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref>
